items.definition_en: A hotel is a building where people can stay when they travel.
items.example_en: We stayed at a nice hotel near the beach.

items.definition_en: A motel is a place to stay, usually by the road, with rooms for travelers.
items.example_en: They rested at a motel during their road trip.

items.definition_en: A hostel is a budget place where many people can sleep in shared rooms.
items.example_en: Sara met new friends while staying at the hostel.

items.definition_en: An apartment is a set of rooms where people can live or stay temporarily.
items.example_en: We rented an apartment for our holiday.

items.definition_en: A guesthouse is a small place where people can stay overnight.
items.example_en: They booked a room at a friendly guesthouse.

items.definition_en: A bed and breakfast is a small place to sleep and have breakfast.
items.example_en: We found a cozy bed and breakfast in the village.

items.definition_en: A villa is a large, comfortable house where people can stay on holiday.
items.example_en: They rented a villa with a swimming pool.

items.definition_en: A campsite is a place where people put up tents when traveling.
items.example_en: The children stayed at a campsite in the forest.

items.definition_en: A caravan is a vehicle you can live in while traveling.
items.example_en: We spent our holiday in a caravan by the lake.

items.definition_en: A lodge is a small house or cabin where travelers can stay.
items.example_en: They slept in a mountain lodge during their trip.

items.definition_en: A resort is a place with many activities where people stay for holidays.
items.example_en: The resort offers swimming and tennis for guests.

items.definition_en: A cabin is a small house made of wood where people can stay.
items.example_en: We rented a cabin in the mountains last winter.

items.definition_en: A bungalow is a small house with one floor where people stay.
items.example_en: Our family stayed in a bungalow near the sea.

items.definition_en: An inn is a small hotel, usually in the countryside.
items.example_en: They had lunch at a country inn on their way home.

items.definition_en: A holiday home is a house you rent or use for a vacation.
items.example_en: They spend every summer at their holiday home.

items.definition_en: A dormitory is a large room where many people sleep, often in hostels.
items.example_en: I shared a dormitory with five travelers last night.

items.definition_en: A treehouse is a small house built in a tree to stay in.
items.example_en: They booked a treehouse for a special holiday.

items.definition_en: A farmstay is when you stay at a working farm during your holiday.
items.example_en: We enjoyed feeding animals during our farmstay.

items.definition_en: A studio apartment is a small one-room flat for staying.
items.example_en: I stayed in a small studio apartment in the city.

items.definition_en: A suite is a set of connected rooms in a hotel.
items.example_en: They enjoyed the large suite with a view of the ocean.
